TSLA: James May Says Tesla Model 3 Overcomplicates Basic Controls, Wiper 'Doesn't Work Properly'. British motoring journalist James May criticized the Tesla Model 3 for overcomplicating basic controls, highlighting issues like the multi-step process for driver assistance settings and the ineffectiveness of the camera-based automatic wipers. While praising its quietness and performance, he expressed dislike for Tesla's association with Elon Musk and noted slow charging speeds. Despite these criticisms, the Tesla Model Y, which shares similarities with the Model 3, was the best-selling vehicle globally from 2023-2025.

Tesla faces deeper U.S. probe over Full Self-Driving safety concerns (TSLA:NASDAQ). Federal safety regulators, specifically the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA), are intensifying their probe into Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) system due to concerns about its performance in adverse roadway conditions. The investigation will focus on FSD's ability to handle issues like sun glare, fog, and dust, which have been linked to crashes and fatalities. The NHTSA's findings could lead to recalls or enforcement actions, potentially impacting Tesla's operations and reputation.

Musk says Tesla may 'tape out' next-generation AI6 chips in December. Elon Musk, CEO of Tesla, has indicated that the company might finalize the design ("tape out") for its next-generation AI6 chips by December. These chips, which Samsung Electronics is expected to produce using an advanced 2-nanometer process by the second half of 2027, are intended for use in Tesla's self-driving cars and humanoid robots. The announcement follows Samsung's $16.5 billion deal to supply AI chips to Tesla.

Musk says SpaceX AI, Tesla will keep ordering Nvidia chips at scale. Elon Musk has stated that his companies, SpaceX AI and Tesla, will continue to place large orders for Nvidia chips. This announcement was made late on Thursday, underscoring the ongoing demand for Nvidia's technology in advanced AI and automotive applications. The statement suggests continued reliance of Musk's ventures on Nvidia for their technological needs.

Forget Tesla — Nvidia And Lucid Are Quietly Building Uber's AI Fleet. Nvidia and Lucid are reportedly collaborating to build an autonomous vehicle fleet for Uber, offering a modular competitor to Tesla's vertically integrated robotaxi strategy. Nvidia provides the AI and computing power, while Lucid is developing autonomy-ready vehicles for scaled deployment. Uber's existing global demand and distribution network are seen as crucial to the success of this partnership, potentially shifting the focus of the robotaxi race from individual car makers to ecosystem builders.

Kurv Yield Premium Strategy Tesla (TSLA) ETF (TSLP) Dividend Yield 2026 & History $TSLP. This article provides a detailed overview of the Kurv Yield Premium Strategy Tesla (TSLA) ETF (TSLP) dividend information. It highlights TSLP's significant annual dividend yield of 46.19% and an annual dividend of $8.96 per share, with the most recent payment being $1.30 per share on January 24, 2025. The content also includes historical dividend data, payment schedules, and a comparison of TSLP's dividend performance against other companies and BATS companies.
TGT: Allworth Financial LP Sells 174,094 Shares of AT&T Inc. $T. Allworth Financial LP significantly reduced its stake in AT&T Inc. by selling 174,094 shares, now holding 1,592,002 shares valued at approximately $44.96 million. The company's recent earnings beat expectations with an EPS of $0.52 and revenue of $33.47 billion, and it provided a positive FY2026 guidance. Analysts currently rate AT&T as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $30.35, despite recent stock performance showing some volatility and institutional ownership around 57.10%.

AIA Group Ltd Boosts Holdings in AT&T Inc. $T. AIA Group Ltd significantly increased its stake in AT&T Inc. during Q3, raising its holdings by 89.9% to 437,086 shares valued at $12.34 million. This move is part of a broader trend, with several other institutional investors also increasing their positions, bringing total institutional ownership to 57.10%. Wall Street analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for AT&T, with an average price target of $30.35, following a recent earnings beat and positive FY2026 guidance.

Target Corp shares jump 6% to 11-month high as company lifts sales forecast after another weak quarter. Shares of Target Corp rose significantly, hitting an 11-month high, despite the company reporting another weak quarter. This jump was primarily driven by Target's increased sales forecast, signaling investor confidence in future performance despite recent challenges.

Target Corp. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day. Target Corp. (TGT) shares dropped 1.46% to $115.05 on Wednesday, marking its third consecutive day of losses. Despite the decline, Target outperformed the broader market, as the S&P 500 Index fell 1.36% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 1.63% during the same trading session.

Target Corp. (TGT) jumps 6.7% as firm turns to AI for growth. Target (TGT) saw its stock jump by 6.7% as the company focuses on artificial intelligence to drive growth. This move indicates a strategic shift towards leveraging technology for future expansion and improved operations.
SBUX: Danske Bank A S Acquires Shares of 524,713 Starbucks Corporation $SBUX. Danske Bank A S has acquired a new stake of 524,713 shares in Starbucks Corporation, valued at approximately $44.39 million. This acquisition is part of broader institutional investor activity, with other large firms like Vanguard Group Inc. and Capital Research Global Investors also adjusting their holdings. Despite recent insider selling, analysts maintain an average "Hold" rating for Starbucks, with various target prices reflecting mixed sentiment regarding the company's turnaround efforts and market challenges.

AIA Group Ltd Has $10.99 Million Stock Position in Starbucks Corporation $SBUX. AIA Group Ltd has significantly increased its stake in Starbucks Corporation, now holding 129,880 shares valued at approximately $10.99 million. This increase comes despite Starbucks facing near-term challenges including a recent RBC downgrade due to labor investment, uncertain margin recovery, heightened shareholder activism regarding union talks, and insider stock sales. Analysts currently give Starbucks a consensus "Hold" rating with an average target price of $103.81, acknowledging positive traffic momentum but also significant headwinds.

Pumpkin Spice Latte Returns: Starbucks Icon Drives Fall Sales Surge in 2026 Amid Evolving Consumer Tastes. Starbucks has relaunched its Pumpkin Spice Latte for 2026, introducing a new non-dairy almond-oat version that aims to meet evolving consumer preferences and health consciousness. The seasonal beverage is expected to drive significant sales growth, particularly in Europe, and positively impact Starbucks' stock performance during Q4. The company is focusing on sustainability, expanded retail presence, and influencer marketing to maximize the PSL's commercial impact.

Starbucks Corp Stock (SBUX) Moved Down by 3.56% on Mar 18: Key Drivers Unveiled. Starbucks Corp (SBUX) stock fell 3.56% on March 18, driven primarily by an analyst downgrade from RBC Capital due to higher labor costs and uncertain savings. Further contributing to the decline are ongoing concerns about company governance, labor disputes, and the stock's valuation near its peak multiple. The company's consideration of a significant office space lease in Nashville amidst layoffs also adds to short-term volatility and investor caution.

Starbucks Plummets 3.5% Intraday: Short-Sellers Pounce on Hidden Catalysts. Starbucks (SBUX) experienced a significant 3.5% intraday drop, falling below key moving averages and flashing bearish technical signals. This decline, coupled with a 6% plummet in the 2X Leverage ETF SBU, highlights increased speculative intensity and profit-taking after a long-term bullish trend. The broader Restaurants sector is also under pressure, with McDonald's (MCD) declining, suggesting potential macroeconomic concerns.
DIS: The Walt Disney Company stock faces streaming headwinds amid theme park recovery signals in 2026. The Walt Disney Company (ISIN: US9314271084) is experiencing mixed signals in its business. While its Disney+ streaming service faces challenges with slowing subscriber growth and competition, its domestic theme parks are showing resilience and increased attendance. German-speaking investors are observing these trends, with particular interest in Eurozone tourism recovery, content licensing, and Disney's strategic shift towards profitability over growth in its streaming segment.

Crashing 51%, 3 Reasons to Buy This Netflix Rival in March and Hold for 5 Years. This article suggests that Walt Disney (DIS) is a compelling investment despite its recent stock performance, trading 51% below its all-time high. The author highlights Disney's improving direct-to-consumer streaming segment, which is now profitable, and its strong experiences division, noting its valuable intellectual property. The current low valuation of Disney stock, at a 62% discount to Netflix's P/E ratio, is presented as a strong reason to buy and hold for the next five years.

New Disney CEO gets quizzed on everything from Walt to Figment. New Disney CEO Josh D'Amaro addressed shareholder concerns at an annual meeting, covering topics such as high theme park prices, the company's disability access policy, and balancing new content with beloved sequels. D'Amaro also discussed Walt Disney's legacy and the ongoing adoration for the character Figment. He emphasized Disney's commitment to innovation, flexibility in pricing, and a thoughtful approach to accessibility.

Walt Disney’s Bengaluru centre triggers revenue risk for IT vendor Globant. Walt Disney's new global capability centre (GCC) in Bengaluru poses a significant revenue risk for its IT vendor Globant, which counts Disney as its largest client, contributing 9% of its 2025 revenue. This move highlights a broader trend where large companies are insourcing technology work, impacting IT service providers. The shift could adversely affect Globant's revenues and operations, particularly given its slowed growth in 2025 and reliance on the media and entertainment sector.

The Walt Disney Company $DIS Shares Sold by GM Advisory Group LLC. GM Advisory Group LLC recently reduced its stake in The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS) by 24.8%, selling 5,016 shares and holding 15,200 shares valued at approximately $1.74 million. Despite this sale, other institutional investors, including Rakuten Investment Management Inc., significantly increased their positions, bringing the total institutional ownership of Disney stock to 65.71%. The article also highlights recent positive developments for Disney, such as its streaming business reaching breakeven and leadership transitions, alongside analyst ratings averaging a "Moderate Buy" with a $135.80 price target.

Disney Believe will be next ship’s name, Josh D’Amaro says. Disney has announced that its next cruise ship will be named the Disney Believe, as revealed by CEO Josh D’Amaro during a shareholder meeting. This is part of a significant expansion for Disney Cruise Line, which aims to grow its fleet to 13 ships by 2031, including new classes of ships and an expansion into Japan. The Disney Believe will feature themes from popular Disney stories like "Encanto," "Frozen," "Snow White," "Moana," and "The Little Mermaid."

Josh D'Amaro Succeeds Bob Iger as Walt Disney Company CEO - News and Statistics. Josh D'Amaro has been appointed CEO of The Walt Disney Company, succeeding Bob Iger after Iger's second tenure. D'Amaro, with over 25 years at Disney, brings a diverse background to the role and will oversee the company's extensive portfolio including theme parks, film production, and streaming services. He takes charge as Disney enjoys box office success and invests in its parks, despite a recent decline in share price and challenges in attracting international guests.

Disney Annual Shareholders Meeting: New CEO Josh D’Amaro Speaks to Company’s Next Chapter of Creativity, Innovation, and Connection. During The Walt Disney Company's 2026 Annual Meeting of Shareholders, new CEO Josh D’Amaro outlined the company’s strategic vision, emphasizing a "One Disney" approach to deliver connected, personalized, and immersive experiences. He highlighted upcoming projects like Toy Story 5, sequels to Lilo & Stitch and Incredibles, and the expansion of Disney+ as a digital centerpiece. D'Amaro also discussed ESPN's sports portfolio, significant investments in Experiences including new cruise ships, and the company's commitment to technology and human creativity.

Josh D’Amaro Becomes CEO of The Walt Disney Company. Josh D’Amaro has officially been named the Chief Executive Officer of The Walt Disney Company, following an extensive search. Dana Walden will serve alongside him as President and Chief Creative Officer. D'Amaro's era is expected to focus on growth through innovation, leveraging the strong performance of Disney Experiences and navigating challenges in media and theatrical releases while exploring AI's potential.
